channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Large box of cardboard, 9th July
item.description:
Abandoned large box of cardboard full of rubbish with visible name and address

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Avenue Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A Potholes problem has been found TEST, 9th July
item.description:
A Potholes problem has been found by Camden Council TEST TEST TEST TEST
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cressy Road, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Coin Street & Aquinas Street, 9th July
item.description:
The roads of Coin Street & Aquinas street are an absolute disgrace for SE1. I’ve seen better tarmac on the moon. Lambeth please prioritise the resurfacing of this beautiful area. Coin street is turning into dust. COME ON LAMBETH.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Coin Street, Waterloo, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bags of Insulation dumped on Pathway, 9th July
item.description:
Dumped rubbish
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 47, Mayplace Road East, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car, 9th July
item.description:
See above - registration number SJ17KVX
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marina Drive, Falconwood, Greater London

item.title: Fly tipping on parking of Elmore House, 9th July
item.description:
The stuff has been there for weeks, it seems as if more gets added to it, beside the rubbish bins at the end of Elmore House's parking lot.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Minet Road, Brixton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 9th July
item.description:
This needs to stop yet again more stuff has been dumped
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mayplace Avenue, Barnes Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sunken manhole/drain on colyers lane, 9th July
item.description:
Resident called to report that there is a sunken drain/manhole outside number 166 colyers lane, when any bus, lorry, van (heavy vehicle) goes over this it makes a really loud noise and shakes the house to the point that the residents bed moves. Resident also stated he doesnt know if this noise is to do with the speed humps? This has been going on for around 4 years now. Please can this be looked into asap.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Colyers Lane, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Heavily littered alleyway, 9th July
item.description:
The alleyway between Silverdale Road and the cul-de-sac of Ravenscourt Road is heavily littered. It has been like this for over a month. My understanding is that it is meant to be cleaned routinely each Thursday but this is not happening. Please could this be cleared and issues preventing routine cleaning be addressed?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leesons Hill, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bags of rubbish on King William Lane, 9th July
item.description:
Bags of rubbish on King William Lane

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): King William Lane, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Failure to collect recycling bins, 9th July
item.description:
After three days and multiple reports to Enfield Council the blue recycling bins remain uncollected in Melbourne Avenue.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Melbourne Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Knocked over street sign, 9th July
item.description:
Dangerous on path

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 308, Westwood Lane, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London

item.title: Overturned salt bin and litter, 9th July
item.description:
Litter needs clear in and salt bin removed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Westwood Lane, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown grass, 9th July
item.description:
Can the grass in this open space be cut. From Glyndon up to where Foxfield primary school is. It is summer time and some of us don’t have gardens and this is an open space that can be used with families/children. at the moment the space cannot be used as it isn’t suitable. Also the grass is tall and dry and hurts when you walk through it and you cannot see if someone’s dog has used the grass without the owner cleaning behind them.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Invermore Place, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pot hole that is increasing in strength ze, 9th July
item.description:
This pot hole has been there since winter. It’s growing and is dangerous to cyclists, pedestrians and motor vehicles.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hansol Road, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken bike and parasol stand dumped, 9th July
item.description:
Flytipping at the end of Linden Grove, next to the single garage just before the alley

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Linden Grove, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped AGAIN on the same spot, 9th July
item.description:
This was reported and cleared 3 days ago and AGAIN rubbish was dumped on the same spot !
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mayplace Avenue, Barnes Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cyclists and motorcyclist speeding through the alleyway, 9th July
item.description:
Unsure how to categorise this report but basically cyclists and motorcyclists are speeding through the alleyway, endangering pedestrians and causing near-misses with people and vehicles. The current central pole is insufficient and a suitable barrier needs to be installed to slow them down on both ends of the path. I’ll leave for you to decide on the type as you’d need to consider those with support needs. Thank you.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 22A,22B,22C,22D, Sholden Gardens, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
